Based on the search results, here's what I found about the CRXJS Vite plugin for Chrome extensions:

## CRXJS Vite Plugin

CRXJS is designed to build cross-browser extensions with native HMR and zero-config setup.

### Key Features
The plugin offers:
- Full Vite Plugin Ecosystem - Leverage any Vite-compatible plugins with zero extra setup
- Zero Configuration - Start developing immediately with intelligent defaults
- Manifest V3 Support - Built for modern Chrome extensions with enhanced security
- True Hot Module Replacement - Instant UI updates while preserving extension state (works with content scripts)
- Static Asset Import - Directly reference images/fonts in your code
- Auto Web-Accessible Resources - Automatic generation of web_accessible_resources manifest entries

### Latest Version & Vite Support
After three years of beta use, from Vite 3 to Vite 6, the plugin has proven its stability for Chrome extension development.(no content)You can quickly scaffold your extension with `npm create crxjs@latest`.

### Recent Updates
The plugin includes enhanced support for developing extensions with Svelte.(no content)Vite 5 moved the vite manifest from 'manifest.json' to '.vite/manifest.json'. The plugin has been updated to use the new location if Vite major version is >4, old location otherwise.

### Project Status (2025)
The CRXJS project is currently seeking new maintainers. As of early 2025, the project operates under maintenance constraints.(no content)The primary active package is @crxjs/vite-plugin for Manifest V3 extensions. A legacy package, rollup-plugin-chrome-extension, supports Manifest V2 extensions but is no longer under active development.

### Resources
- **npm**: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@crxjs/vite-plugin
- **GitHub**: https://github.com/crxjs/chrome-extension-tools
- **Documentation**: https://crxjs.dev/vite-plugin/

Setup with React 19, Typescript and TailwindCSS","url":"https://github.com/JohnBra/vite-web-extension"},{"title":"Create Browser Extension with Vite + TS - DEV Community","url":"https://dev.to/ivan_862363c9a8b0/create-browser-extension-with-vite-ts-3d61"},{"title":"Building a Chrome Extension with Vite, React, and Tailwind CSS in 2025","url":"https://www.artmann.co/articles/building-a-chrome-extension-with-vite-react-and-tailwind-css-in-2025"},{"title":"Build a Chrome Extension with React, TypeScript and Vite - DEV Community","url":"https://dev.to/nefayran/build-a-chrome-extension-with-react-typescript-and-vite-31ho"},{"title":"Chrome Extensions using Vite + Typescript + React: Stepwise Process - Single Quote","url":"https://singlequote.blog/chrome-extension-using-vite-typescript-react-stepwise-process/"},{"title":"Creating a Chrome Extension with React and Vite (Boilerplate provided) | by Chris Oh | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@5tigerjelly/creating-a-chrome-extension-with-react-and-vite-boilerplate-provided-db3d14473bf6"},{"title":"Vite Plugin Web Extension | Modern Web Extension Development","url":"https://vite-plugin-web-extension.aklinker1.io/"},{"title":"Chrome Extensions using Vite + Typescript + React: Stepwise Process | by Ashish Mishra | Medium","url":"https://arglee.medium.com/chrome-extensions-using-vite-typescript-react-stepwise-process-6d013f5332b9"},{"title":"Chrome Extensions using Vite + Typescript + React: Stepwise Process - DEV Community","url":"https://dev.to/arglee/chrome-extensions-using-vite-typescript-react-stepwise-process-2ddp"}]

Here's what I found about setting up Chrome extension development with Vite and TypeScript in 2025:

## Popular Approaches and Boilerplates

### 1. **Ready-to-Use Boilerplates**

**chrome-extension-boilerplate-react-vite** (GitHub)
This boilerplate helps you create Chrome/Firefox extensions using React and Typescript. It improves the build speed and development experience by using Vite and Turborepo.(no content)It includes content scripts, content-ui (React components injected into pages), devtools panel, popup, and side-panel support (Chrome 114+).

**vite-web-extension** (GitHub - JohnBra)
This boilerplate is meant to be a minimal quick start for creating chrome/firefox extensions using React, Typescript and Tailwind CSS.(no content)You can build dist files for both Chrome and Firefox with manifest v3.

### 2. **Quick Setup from Scratch**

To initialize a new Vite project with React and TypeScript support, use: `npx create vite@latest --template react-ts my-chrome-extension`(no content)Nowadays, many developers use Bun to run TypeScript code. If you want to use Node with yarn or npm, using Node 23 will make your life easier as it comes with built-in TypeScript support.

### 3. **TypeScript Chrome API Types**

To get TypeScript support for Chrome APIs, install @types/chrome to provide the necessary type definitions for the chrome object. Once installed, the TypeScript errors disappear.

### 4. **Vite Plugin for Web Extensions**

The vite-plugin-web-extension automatically launches Chrome or Firefox with the extension installed, supports Hot Module Replacement (HMR) for quick UI iterations, and allows adding any frontend framework's Vite plugin to use in your extension.(no content)It offers first-class, out-of-the-box TypeScript support without the need for additional configuration.

### 5. **Key Configuration Notes**

Vite normally builds files with absolute paths, but Chrome extensions need relative paths to work properly, so you'll need to fix the Vite config to make it extension-friendly.

### 6. **Loading the Extension**

After building, this creates a dist/ folder with your extension files. Open Chrome and navigate to chrome://extensions, enable "Developer mode" in the top right corner, click "Load unpacked" and select the dist/ folder.

### Stack Summary

A typical modern stack for Chrome extensions built with Vite includes: React 18, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind CSS, and Manifest V3.

Links: [{"title":"Migrate to Manifest V3 | Chrome for Developers","url":"https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/develop/migrate"},{"title":"Extensions / Manifest V3 | Chrome for Developers","url":"https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/develop/migrate/what-is-mv3"},{"title":"What Manifest V3 means for Brave Shields and the use of extensions in the Brave browser | Brave","url":"https://brave.com/blog/brave-shields-manifest-v3/"},{"title":"GitHub - dipankar/chrome-extension-best-practices: These are the best practices for Manifest V3 browser extensions","url":"https://github.com/dipankar/chrome-extension-best-practices"},{"title":"Resuming the transition to Manifest V3 | Blog | Chrome for Developers","url":"https://developer.chrome.com/blog/resuming-the-transition-to-mv3/"},{"title":"What's new in Chrome extensions | Chrome Extensions | Chrome for Developers","url":"https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/whats-new"},{"title":"Chrome Extension Development Guide 2025: From Idea to Chrome Store | Diego Rodriguez","url":"https://www.diego-rodriguez.work/blog/chrome-extension-development-guide"},{"title":"Best Practices | Chrome Extensions | Chrome for Developers","url":"https://developer.chrome.com/docs/webstore/best-practices"},{"title":"Migrate extensions to Manifest V3 before June 2025 ? - Chrome Enterprise & Education Community","url":"https://support.google.com/chrome/a/thread/325886471/migrate-extensions-to-manifest-v3-before-june-2025?hl=en"},{"title":"Mastering Chrome Extension Manifest v3: A Step-by-Step Tutorial for Developers - Coded Brainy","url":"https://www.codedbrainy.com/mastering-chrome-extension-manifest-v3-a-step-by-step-tutorial-for-developers/"}]

Here are the search results for Chrome extension Manifest V3 best practices in 2025:

## Key Findings

### Migration Timeline
In June 2025, Google plans to remove all remaining Manifest V2 items from the Chrome Web Store.(no content)Manifest V3 is the most recent version of the Chrome extension platform and is the required version for submitting new items to the Chrome Web Store.

### Core Architecture Changes
In Manifest V3, the background context has moved to service workers, which run only when needed. Manifest V3 also removes the ability for an extension to use remotely hosted code, which presents security risks by allowing unreviewed code to be executed in extensions.(no content)Manifest v3 introduces a new architecture for extensions designed to improve security, privacy, and performance. It also introduces a new permissions system that is more granular and restricts the access of extensions to sensitive user data.

### Migration Best Practices
To reduce the chances of unexpected issues or bugs, it's recommended not to add new functionality when migrating. For instance, adding a feature that requires new permissions may trigger a permission warning, which will disable your extension until the user accepts the new permissions.(no content)There is an Extension Manifest Converter available. It does not do everything for you, but it will get you started. The converter's README describes what the tool changes.

### Platform Improvements
As a result of developer feedback, Chrome has made several changes to Manifest V3 including: introducing Offscreen Documents for DOM access, providing better control over service worker lifetimes, adding a new User Scripts API, and improving content filtering support with more generous limits in the declarativeNetRequest API.(no content)Beginning in Chrome 120, Manifest V3 extensions can use the chrome.alarms API with delays or periods of 30 seconds, rather than requiring values of 60 seconds or more.

### Development Best Practices
Security best practice: Use the extension ID for message passing to ensure that only your extension can communicate with its components. Modularity: Separate concerns into different files (e.g., background.js, content.js, storage.js) for better organization and maintainability.(no content)Your extension should be safe for your users. For example, send user data securely via HTTPS or web services security. Check that your extension does not pose security threats and does not use deceptive installation tactics.(no content)An extension is required to disclose in the Privacy tab what user data it will collect and how it will handle user data. This information must be accurate, up-to-date, and match the extension's privacy policy.

### New APIs Available
The Side Panel API is a companion surface that allows users to access tools alongside the content they are browsing.(no content)The Extension Update Testing Tool has been released - a local extension update server that can be used for testing updates to Chrome Extensions during local development, including permission grants. This tool is particularly useful for simulating the permission changes requested when updating an extension from Manifest V2 to Manifest V3.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Based on my search results, here's the latest information about TypeScript 5.7:

## TypeScript 5.7 Version Information

The latest stable version of TypeScript 5.7 is **5.7.3**. The 5.7 release line includes TypeScript 5.7.0 (Beta), 5.7.1 (RC), 5.7.2 (Stable), and 5.7.3 (Stable).(no content)The current latest version of TypeScript overall on npm is 5.9.3.

## Key Features in TypeScript 5.7

TypeScript 5.7 introduces a range of new features and improvements designed to enhance developer productivity and code reliability, focusing on refining variable initialization checks, improving module import handling, supporting the latest ECMAScript features, and optimizing project configuration resolution in editors.(no content)TypeScript 5.7 enhanced checks to catch uninitialized variables even when accessed within nested functions, which was a limitation in previous versions.(no content)Node.js 22 supports a new API called `module.enableCompileCache()`, and TypeScript 5.7 leverages this API so it can start doing useful work sooner. In testing, they witnessed about a 2.5x speed-up in running `tsc --version`.(no content)In ECMAScript 2024, SharedArrayBuffer and ArrayBuffer have types that slightly diverge. To bridge the gap, all TypedArrays (like Uint8Array and others) are now also generic with a type parameter named TArrayBuffer.

## Current TypeScript Roadmap

Much of the TypeScript team's recent focus has been on the native port of TypeScript which will eventually be available as TypeScript 7.0. TypeScript 6.0 is envisioned as a transition point for developers to adjust their codebases for TypeScript 7.0.(no content)TypeScript 7 is close to making builds feel instantaneous for smaller changes in large projects, and often sees close to a 10x speedup over the 6.0 compiler on full builds.

Based on the search results, here's the information about `vite-plugin-web-extension`:

## Latest Version

The latest version is **4.4.5**, which was published 3 months ago.

## Important Note About Deprecation

vite-plugin-web-extension will soon be deprecated in favor of WXT, its successor.(no content)While WXT is still in pre-release, it already supports everything this plugin does, is significantly more popular, and includes more features and bug fixes that won't be added to vite-plugin-web-extension. If you're starting a new project, the author recommends using WXT instead.(no content)Once WXT v1.0 is released, the author will provide a more in-depth migration guide for existing projects and vite-plugin-web-extension will enter maintenance mode.

## Installation

You can create a new project using:
```bash
pnpm create vite-plugin-web-extension
npm create vite-plugin-web-extension
yarn create vite-plugin-web-extension
```

## Key Features

- Automatically launches Chrome or Firefox with the extension installed. Supports Hot Module Replacement (HMR) for quick UI iterations and a watch mode to rebuild content scripts upon changes.
- Supports choice of Manifest version, TypeScript support, and framework (Preact, React, Solid, Svelte, Vanilla, Vue).

Note: There are two different packages with similar names:
1. `vite-plugin-web-extension` (by aklinker1) - version 4.4.5
2. `@samrum/vite-plugin-web-extension` (by samrum) - a separate package

Links: [{"title":"vite - npm","url":"https://www.npmjs.com/package/vite"},{"title":"Releases | Vite","url":"https://vite.dev/releases"},{"title":"Vite 6.0 is out! | Vite","url":"https://vite.dev/blog/announcing-vite6"},{"title":"Getting Started | Vite","url":"https://vite.dev/guide/"},{"title":"create-vite - npm","url":"https://www.npmjs.com/package/create-vite?activeTab=versions"},{"title":"Vite 7.0 is out! | Vite","url":"https://vite.dev/blog/announcing-vite7"},{"title":"Migration from v6 | Vite","url":"https://vite.dev/guide/migration"},{"title":"npm | Profile","url":"https://www.npmjs.com/org/vitejs"},{"title":"Releases · vitejs/vite","url":"https://github.com/vitejs/vite/releases"},{"title":"@vitejs/plugin-react - npm","url":"https://www.npmjs.com/package/@vitejs/plugin-react"}]

Based on my search results, here's what I found about Vite versions:

**Current Status (December 2025):**

Vite 7 is now out and should be a smooth update from Vite 6. It removes already deprecated features, like Sass legacy API support and the splitVendorChunkPlugin.(no content)The latest stable Vite version is 7.2.6 (published 4 days ago), with version 7.2.10 published 2 days ago.

**Vite 6 Information:**

Vite 6 was released on November 26, 2024, as announced by the Vite team, contributors, and ecosystem partners.(no content)Vite 6 supports Node.js 18, 20, and 22+, similar to Vite 5. Node.js 21 support was dropped.(no content)Vite 6 introduced the new Environment API, allowing framework authors to offer a dev experience closer to production and for the Ecosystem to share new building blocks.

**Vite 7 Changes:**

Vite 7 now requires Node.js 20.19+ or 22.12+, having dropped Node.js 18 after it reached EOL at the end of April 2025.(no content)In Vite 7, the default browser target changed from 'modules' to a new default: 'baseline-widely-available'.

So while you asked about Vite 6, note that **Vite 7 is now the current major version** as of December 2025, with 7.2.x being the latest release series.

Based on the search results, here's the information about WXT (Web Extension Framework):

## WXT - Next-gen Web Extension Framework

WXT is a next-gen framework for developing web extensions.(no content)It's described as being "like Nuxt, but for Web Extensions."

### Latest Version
The latest version is **0.20.11**, last published about 3 months ago on npm.(no content)The recent release v0.20.10 included upgrades to non-breaking production dependencies, web-ext-run to support CDP, and publish-browser-extension upgrades.

### Key Features

WXT uses Vite under the hood to provide features like HMR (Hot Module Replacement), which is crucial when dealing with complex web extensions. WXT allows you to use any framework of your choice, including React, Svelte, Vue, and more.(no content)WXT is written in TypeScript and has 8.6k stars on GitHub.(no content)WXT simplifies the web extension development process by providing tools for zipping and publishing, best-in-class dev mode, and an opinionated project structure. It can automatically zip, upload, submit, and publish extensions, and works with any front-end framework with a Vite plugin.

### 2025 Status

Analysis indicates that WXT has established itself as the definitive leading framework for modern browser extension development. This leadership position is founded on its superior developer experience, a robust and flexible feature set, a framework-agnostic architecture, and a track record of active and reliable open-source maintenance.(no content)WXT's viability is strongly supported by its widespread adoption and active community. The framework's official website showcases popular, production-ready extensions built with WXT, including "Eye Dropper" (1,000,000+ users) and "ChatGPT Writer" (600,000+ users).

### Getting Started

You can start using WXT in your project by running `npm i wxt`.

{
  "extends": "./.wxt/tsconfig.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ESNext",
    "useDefineForClassFields": true,
    "module": "ESNext",
    "lib": ["ESNext", "DOM", "DOM.Iterable"],
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "allowImportingTsExtensions": true,
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "noEmit": true,
    "strict": true,
    "noUnusedLocals": true,
    "noUnusedParameters": true,
    "noFallthroughCasesInSwitch": true,
    "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true
  },
  "include": [
    "entrypoints/**/*",
    "components/**/*",
    "utils/**/*",
    "scripts/**/*"
  ],
  "exclude": ["node_modules", "dist", ".output", ".wxt"]
}

input.content:
# Video Downloader Extension

A Chrome extension for downloading videos from web pages, built with TypeScript, Vite, and WXT framework.

> **Disclaimer**: This extension is intended for personal use and study purposes only. Please respect copyright laws and terms of service of the websites you visit.

## Features

- Detect HTML5 video elements on web pages
- Extract videos from Open Graph and meta tags
- Parse JSON-LD structured data for video content
- Simple popup UI showing all detected videos
- One-click download with automatic filename generation
- Badge indicator showing video count per tab

## Tech Stack

- **Framework**: [WXT](https://wxt.dev/) - Next-gen Web Extension Framework
- **Build Tool**: [Vite 7](https://vite.dev/)
- **Language**: [TypeScript 5.7+](https://www.typescriptlang.org/)
- **Manifest**: Chrome Extension Manifest V3

## Prerequisites

- Node.js 20.19+ or 22.12+
- npm, yarn, or pnpm
- Google Chrome browser

## Installation

1. Clone the repository:

```bash
git clone <repository-url>
cd video-downloader-extension
```

2. Install dependencies:

```bash
npm install
```

3. Generate icons:

```bash
npm run icons
```

## Development

### Start Development Server

Run the extension in development mode with hot module replacement:

```bash
npm run dev
```

This will:
- Build the extension
- Open Chrome with the extension loaded
- Enable HMR for instant updates

### Build for Production

```bash
npm run build
```

The built extension will be in `.output/chrome-mv3/`.

### Auto-Install in Chrome

For quick testing, use the install script:

```bash
npm run chrome:install
```

This will:
1. Build the extension
2. Create a temporary Chrome profile
3. Launch Chrome with the extension pre-loaded

To start with a clean Chrome profile:

```bash
npm run chrome:install:clean
```

### Firefox Support

```bash
# Development
npm run dev:firefox

# Build
npm run build:firefox

# Package
npm run zip:firefox
```

## Project Structure

```
video-downloader-extension/
├── entrypoints/           # Extension entry points
│   ├── background.ts      # Service worker
│   ├── content.ts         # Content script
│   └── popup/             # Popup UI
│       ├── index.html
│       ├── main.ts
│       └── style.css
├── public/                # Static assets
│   └── icon/              # Extension icons
├── utils/                 # Shared utilities
│   └── video-detector.ts  # Video detection logic
├── scripts/               # Build/dev scripts
│   ├── generate-icons.js  # Icon generator
│   └── install-chrome.js  # Chrome auto-installer
├── wxt.config.ts          # WXT configuration
├── tsconfig.json          # TypeScript config
└── package.json
```

## Available Scripts

| Script | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `npm run dev` | Start development server |
| `npm run dev:firefox` | Start dev server for Firefox |
| `npm run build` | Build for Chrome production |
| `npm run build:firefox` | Build for Firefox production |
| `npm run zip` | Create Chrome extension package |
| `npm run zip:firefox` | Create Firefox extension package |
| `npm run compile` | Type-check without emitting |
| `npm run icons` | Generate extension icons |
| `npm run chrome:install` | Build and install in Chrome |
| `npm run chrome:install:clean` | Clean install in Chrome |
| `npm run test` | Run tests in watch mode |
| `npm run test:run` | Run tests once |

## How It Works

### Video Detection

The extension uses multiple strategies to detect videos:

1. **HTML5 Video Elements**: Scans for `<video>` tags and `<source>` elements
2. **Open Graph Tags**: Checks `og:video` and related meta tags
3. **Twitter Cards**: Parses `twitter:player:stream` meta tags
4. **JSON-LD**: Extracts `VideoObject` from structured data

### Download Process

1. User clicks the extension icon
2. Content script scans the page for videos
3. Popup displays detected videos with metadata
4. User clicks download button
5. Background service worker initiates download via Chrome Downloads API

## Permissions

| Permission | Purpose |
|------------|---------|
| `activeTab` | Access current tab to detect videos |
| `downloads` | Download video files |
| `storage` | Store user preferences |
| `host_permissions: <all_urls>` | Detect videos on any website |

## Contributing

1. Fork the repository
2. Create a feature branch
3. Make your changes
4. Run tests: `npm run test:run`
5. Run type check: `npm run compile`
6. Submit a pull request

## License

MIT License - see [LICENSE](LICENSE) for details.

## Resources

- [WXT Documentation](https://wxt.dev/)
- [Chrome Extension Docs](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/)
- [Manifest V3 Migration](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/develop/migrate)
- [Vite Documentation](https://vite.dev/)

content.text:
A extensão foi criada com sucesso! Aqui está o resumo:

## Video Downloader Extension

Extensão Chrome criada em `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/video-downloader-extension/`

### Stack Utilizada
- **WXT 0.20+** - Framework de extensões recomendado em 2025 (sucessor do vite-plugin-web-extension)
- **Vite 7** - Build tool mais recente
- **TypeScript 5.7+** - Versão atual
- **Manifest V3** - Padrão obrigatório desde junho 2025

### Estrutura do Projeto
```
video-downloader-extension/
├── entrypoints/
│   ├── background.ts      # Service worker
│   ├── content.ts         # Detecção de vídeos
│   └── popup/             # UI do popup
├── utils/video-detector.ts # Lógica de detecção
├── scripts/
│   ├── generate-icons.js  # Gerador de ícones
│   └── install-chrome.js  # Auto-instalação
└── wxt.config.ts
```

### Comandos Principais
| Comando | Descrição |
|---------|-----------|
| `npm run dev` | Desenvolvimento com HMR |
| `npm run build` | Build de produção |
| `npm run chrome:install` | **Instala automaticamente no Chrome** |
| `npm run icons` | Gera ícones |

### Para Começar
```bash
cd video-downloader-extension
npm install
npm run icons       # Gera ícones
npm run chrome:install  # Build + abre Chrome com a extensão
```

### Fontes de Pesquisa
- [WXT Framework](https://wxt.dev/)
- [Vite 7 Releases](https://vite.dev/releases)
- [Chrome Manifest V3 Best Practices](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/develop/migrate)
- [TypeScript 5.7 Announcement](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/typescript/announcing-typescript-5-7/)

Testes concluídos com sucesso usando **Bun 1.3.3**:

```
✔ bun install     → 382 packages em 2.82s
✔ bun run icons   → 4 ícones PNG gerados
✔ bun run build   → Build em 306ms (24.53 kB total)
✔ bun run compile → Type check passou
✔ bun run dev     → Chrome abriu com a extensão carregada
```

### Resultados do Build
```
.output/chrome-mv3/
├── manifest.json               552 B
├── popup.html                  1.6 kB
├── background.js               2.16 kB
├── chunks/popup-D_AkiMEI.js    4.93 kB
├── content-scripts/content.js  7.3 kB
├── assets/popup-Clpj9vQP.css   3.89 kB
└── icon/                       ~4 kB
```

### Commits
1. `4d5fb6e` - Initial commit
2. `c5253bb` - Fix WXT configuration and add bun support

A extensão está pronta para uso! Para testar:
```bash
cd video-downloader-extension
bun install
bun run dev   # Abre Chrome com a extensão
```

content.text:
O Chrome abriu com a extensão. Agora você pode testar:

1. **YouTube** - Vá a um vídeo e clique no ícone da extensão
2. **Instagram** - Vá a um reel/post com vídeo
3. **Twitter/X** - Vá a um tweet com vídeo

A extensão agora:
- Intercepta requests de rede para capturar URLs de vídeo
- Analisa o DOM e scripts da página para sites específicos (Instagram, Twitter, TikTok)
- Combina resultados de múltiplas fontes

**Importante sobre YouTube**: O YouTube usa streaming adaptativo (DASH), então os vídeos são divididos em segmentos. A extensão vai interceptar as URLs do `googlevideo.com`, mas fazer download deles diretamente pode resultar em apenas um segmento. Para YouTube completo, geralmente é necessário usar ferramentas como `yt-dlp`.

Teste e me diga o resultado!

content.text:
Pronto! As melhorias foram implementadas:

## Mudanças Aplicadas

### Filtragem de Vídeos
- **Skip init segments**: `dashinit.mp4`, `init.mp4`, `.m3u8`, `.mpd`, `.ts`, `.m4s`
- **Instagram específico**: Verifica `duration_s` na URL para identificar vídeos completos (não segmentos)

### Extração de Títulos
- **Instagram**: Extrai `@username` + caption do `og:description`
- **Twitter/X**: Pega texto do tweet
- **TikTok**: Pega descrição do vídeo
- Limpa sufixos como "- YouTube", "| Instagram"

### Nomes de Arquivo
- **Slugify**: `@usuario - Isso é um teste!` → `usuario-isso-e-um-teste.mp4`

### Status de Download
- Mostra "Downloaded" com botão verde para vídeos já baixados
- "Downloading..." durante o download
- "Failed" / "Retry" em caso de erro
- Vídeos baixados ficam com opacidade reduzida

### Thumbnails
- Extrai de `og:image`, `twitter:image`, ou `video[poster]`

Para testar:
```bash
source ~/.zshrc; bun run dev
```

Navegue ao Instagram e teste um reel. Agora deve aparecer apenas 1-4 vídeos (não 64) com títulos reais e thumbnails.

content.text: Vejo o problema. A função `isFullVideo` está muito restritiva para o Instagram - se não encontrar `duration_s` nem `progressive`, retorna `false` implicitamente pelo `return true` no final que só é alcançado se não for Instagram. Vou corrigir:

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The conversation is summarized below:
Analysis:
Let me chronologically analyze the conversation:

1. **Initial Request**: User asked to create a Chrome extension for downloading videos using TypeScript, Vite, and best practices. Requirements included:
   - Research latest package versions (December 9, 2025)
   - Auto-install script for Chrome
   - Git repository with proper commits
   - Code and documentation in English

2. **Research Phase**: I searched for:
   - Vite 7 (current version)
   - TypeScript 5.7+
   - WXT framework (recommended successor to vite-plugin-web-extension)
   - Chrome Manifest V3 best practices

3. **Initial Project Setup**:
   - Created project at `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/video-downloader-extension/`
   - Set up WXT framework, TypeScript, Vite
   - Created video-detector.ts, content.ts, background.ts, popup UI
   - Created auto-install script

4. **First Issues**: WXT config was wrong (`srcDir: 'entrypoints'` should be `srcDir: '.'` with `entrypointsDir: 'entrypoints'`). Also `runner` was deprecated, needed `webExt`.

5. **User Feedback - Testing with Bun**: User asked to test with bunjs. Fixed configs, ran `bun install`, `bun run icons`, `bun run build`, `bun run dev` - all worked.

6. **User Feedback - YouTube/Instagram Issues**:
   - "no youtube baixou um html, no instagram nao detectou nada"
   - YouTube uses DASH streaming (blob URLs)
   - Instagram protects videos

7. **Major Improvements Made**:
   - Added network request interception in background.ts
   - Added site-specific detection for Instagram, Twitter, TikTok
   - Added webRequest permission

8. **User Feedback - Instagram showing 64 videos**:
   - "No instagram, a extensao mostra '64' para uma pagina de reel"
   - Most were init segments (dashinit.mp4) that QuickTime couldn't play
   - Only one worked (instagram.mp4)
   - No thumbnails shown
   - Needed slugify for filenames

9. **Second Major Improvement**:
   - Added filtering for init segments (dashinit, .m3u8, .mpd, .ts, .m4s)
   - Added `isFullVideo()` function checking `duration_s` parameter
   - Added slugify for filenames
   - Added page title extraction from og:description
   - Added thumbnail extraction
   - Added download status tracking (downloading/completed/failed)

10. **Current Issue (most recent)**: User reported:
    - "No youtube baixou (short) mas nao funcionou, exibiu dois com nome 'playback.mp4'"
    - "no instagram parou de detectar"
    
The Instagram issue is likely because `isFullVideo()` returns false for Instagram URLs that don't have `duration_s` or `progressive` in them - it's too restrictive now.

Key files modified:
- `wxt.config.ts` - WXT configuration
- `utils/video-detector.ts` - Video detection logic
- `entrypoints/background.ts` - Background service worker with network interception
- `entrypoints/popup/main.ts` - Popup UI
- `entrypoints/popup/style.css` - Styles
- `entrypoints/content.ts` - Content script

Current state: The Instagram filtering is too aggressive, blocking valid videos. YouTube shorts shows "playback.mp4" as title.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   User requested creation of a Chrome extension for downloading videos (personal use/study) with:
   - TypeScript, Vite, and best practices
   - Latest package versions (December 2025)
   - Auto-install script for Chrome testing
   - Git repository with proper commits
   - Code and documentation in English
   - Support for YouTube, Instagram, Twitter, TikTok
   - Slugified filenames, thumbnails, download status tracking
   - Filter out invalid video segments

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - WXT 0.20+ framework (successor to vite-plugin-web-extension)
   - Vite 7.2.7, TypeScript 5.9.3
   - Chrome Extension Manifest V3

Done! Here's what I fixed:

## Fixes Applied

### 1. Instagram Detection (was too restrictive)
- **File**: `entrypoints/background.ts:196-214`
- **Problem**: Instagram URLs without `duration_s` or `progressive` were being blocked
- **Fix**: Changed the default return from `false` to `true` for URLs that pass the `dashinit` check

### 2. YouTube Shorts Title (was showing "playback.mp4")
- **Files**: 
  - `utils/video-detector.ts:38-65` - Added YouTube-specific title extraction
  - `entrypoints/content.ts:8-75` - Added `getPageTitle()` function with site-specific logic
  - `entrypoints/popup/main.ts:69-88` - Updated to use page title for videos with generic names
  - `entrypoints/background.ts:312-328` - Skip generic names like "playback", "video", "media"

### 3. Added Test Suite
- **File**: `test/video-detector.test.ts` - 15 unit tests for utility functions

## Commands to Test
```bash
# Build extension
~/.bun/bin/bun run build

# Run tests
~/.bun/bin/bun run test:run

# Start dev mode
~/.bun/bin/bun run dev
```

The extension is built and ready in `.output/chrome-mv3/`. Load it in Chrome via `chrome://extensions` with developer mode enabled.

content.text:
Build e testes passaram. As mudanças que fiz:

## Correções para Instagram

1. **VIDEO_PATTERNS ampliado** (`background.ts:31-39`):
   - `cdninstagram\.com` agora aceita qualquer URL do CDN (não só as com "video")
   - Adicionado `scontent.*instagram` para CDN alternativo

2. **Validação melhorada** (`background.ts:197-219` e `video-detector.ts:204-236`):
   - URLs do Instagram precisam ter `.mp4`
   - Bloqueamos `dashinit` (segmentos de inicialização)
   - Se tiver `duration_s > 1` ou `progressive`, aceita
   - Outros `.mp4` são aceitos por padrão

Recarregue a extensão no Chrome (`chrome://extensions` > botão de reload) e teste novamente no Instagram.

content.text:
Pronto! Agora você tem testes E2E com Playwright. Aqui estão os comandos disponíveis:

## Comandos de Teste E2E

```bash
# Teste rápido de saúde da extensão
~/.bun/bin/bun run test:e2e -- --grep "quick extension health check"

# Teste rápido no Instagram (abre browser, vai pro instagram, verifica popup)
~/.bun/bin/bun run test:e2e -- --grep "Instagram quick check"

# Teste interativo/manual - abre browser por 5 minutos para você testar
~/.bun/bin/bun run test:e2e -- --grep "manual Instagram test"

# Rodar todos os testes E2E
~/.bun/bin/bun run test:e2e

# Debug mode (pausa nos breakpoints)
~/.bun/bin/bun run test:e2e:debug
```

## Teste com URL específica do Instagram

```bash
# Passar URL de um reel específico
INSTAGRAM_REEL_URL="https://www.instagram.com/reel/ABC123/" npx playwright test --grep "detects videos on Instagram reel"
```

message.content: Espere, vou testar, no seu teste foi aberto uma nova aba de popup, nao sei se funcionaria da mesma forma, voce sabe?

content.text:
Você está certo em questionar isso. O popup da extensão normalmente é aberto clicando no ícone da extensão, não numa aba separada. Quando abrimos `chrome-extension://ID/popup.html` numa aba, ele funciona como uma página normal, mas **não tem acesso à aba ativa atual** - porque a "aba ativa" seria o próprio popup.

Isso explica porque o popup mostra "no videos" - ele não consegue detectar a aba do Instagram.

Deixe-me verificar se há uma forma de testar isso corretamente. O Playwright tem limitações com popups de extensões, mas podemos:

1. **Usar a API do background script diretamente** - chamar as funções via `chrome.runtime`
2. **Simular o clique no ícone da extensão** - mais complexo

Vou criar um teste que injeta código na página do Instagram para verificar diretamente se os vídeos estão sendo interceptados:

content.content: File created successfully at: /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/video-downloader-extension/e2e/instagram-debug.spec.ts

input.command: INSTAGRAM_REEL_URL="https://www.instagram.com/reels/DIQ7_K6RHcW/" npx playwright test --grep "debug Instagram video interception" 2>&1

content.text: Entendi, vou implementar o método que simula o clique no ícone da extensão. O Playwright permite isso através de `context.pages()` combinado com a abertura do popup real:

content.content: File created successfully at: /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/video-downloader-extension/e2e/extension-popup.spec.ts

content.text:
Agora vou rodar o teste interativo para você testar manualmente. Ele vai:
1. Abrir o Chrome com a extensão carregada
2. Navegar para o reel do Instagram
3. Mostrar os vídeos MP4 detectados via rede
4. Manter o browser aberto para você clicar na extensão e testar

sleep 5

content.text:
O browser deve ter aberto no seu Mac. Você pode:

1. **Ver o reel do Instagram** na aba que abriu
2. **Clicar no ícone de extensões** (quebra-cabeça) no canto superior direito do Chrome
3. **Clicar em "Video Downloader"** para abrir o popup
4. **Verificar se os vídeos aparecem**

No terminal você verá logs como `[VIDEO] https://...` para cada MP4 detectado via rede.

Quando terminar de testar, me avise o que aconteceu - se detectou ou não os vídeos. Posso verificar a saída do teste:

content.text:
Vejo que as URLs têm padrões como:
- `t16/f2/m69` - provavelmente segmentos de streaming
- `t2/f2/m82` - provavelmente segmentos de streaming  
- `t2/f2/m367` - provavelmente segmentos de streaming

Nenhuma dessas URLs tem `video` no path nem `.mp4` na extensão. Isso significa que na versão anterior, essas URLs **não eram capturadas** porque exigiam `/cdninstagram\.com.*?video/`.

O vídeo que funcionou (`instagram.mp4`) deve ter vindo de outra fonte - provavelmente do **content script** que extrai URLs do JSON da página.

Deixe-me reverter o filtro para a versão anterior e também verificar o que o content script do Instagram extrai:

content.text: Agora vou reverter também o video-detector.ts para a versão original e rebuildar:

lines: // Instagram: Check for duration_s in the URL (indicates full video, not init)
// Skip dashinit files - these are initialization segments, not full videos
// Check for duration parameter which indicates it's a real video
return duration > 1; // Must be longer than 1 second
// If URL has progressive in it, it's likely a full video
-    // For other Instagram .mp4 URLs that passed dashinit check, allow them

O browser deve ter aberto. Agora o código está igual à versão que funcionava (`7c81dec`).

Teste clicando na extensão e veja se detecta o vídeo. O comportamento esperado:
- Os vídeos de streaming (segmentos t16, t2, etc) **não** devem aparecer
- Apenas vídeos com `progressive` ou `duration_s` no URL devem aparecer

Avise-me o resultado!

Entendi. Então o problema é que o Instagram mudou e agora não usa mais URLs com `progressive` ou `duration_s`. Precisamos analisar as URLs reais que estão sendo capturadas para encontrar um padrão que identifique vídeos válidos.

## Análise das URLs do Instagram

### 3. **Turbo Downloader for Instagram**
While browsing Instagram, just hover over a post preview and click the download button to save it. If there are multiple images/videos in the post, they'll all be saved. If you're looking at a post or Reel, check next to the bookmark and share icons for the download button, or try the hotkey Ctrl + Shift + D or Cmd + Shift + D.

### 4. **InSaverify (Web for Instagram)**
Using this Instagram extension, you can download Instagram stories, photos, and reels with just one click. Moreover, you can browse IG in a user-friendly environment. InSaverify has more than 800,000 users and a 4.5 rating from almost 800 votes.

### 5. **InsSaver**
InsSaver is a Chrome extension that allows users to download and save Instagram reels, videos, photos, and stories with just one click. This tool supports high-quality downloads from various IG pages, ensuring that users get the best quality content available. It offers easy and fast downloads.

### 6. **Downloader for Instagram**
Downloader for Instagram is a powerful tool that enhances your Instagram experience by allowing you to download photos, videos, stories, and reels from Instagram. With a simple click in your browser, you can access Instagram's features.

### 7. **QuickSave for Instagram**
This extension allows users to effortlessly save Instagram videos, photos, stories, and reels directly to their PC. By adding two new buttons under each post on Instagram - 'Download' and 'Repost' - users can easily download and share content.

**Note:** Always ensure you respect copyright and have permission to download content before saving videos from Instagram.

# Instagram-Downloader: Video Detection & Download Architecture

Based on the repository overview, the main implementation files are located in the `/src` directory, though specific file names aren't detailed in the provided content.

## Key Technical Approach

The extension uses a sophisticated detection mechanism: **"With regex and some `ReactFiber` magic, I'm able to know which post you wanna download"** (HOAIAN2, README).

This indicates two core components:

1. **ReactFiber Integration** - Accesses Instagram's internal React component state to identify posts without requiring DOM manipulation or page-specific selectors that break frequently

2. **Regex Pattern Matching** - Likely processes URLs and page content to distinguish between different Instagram content types (posts, reels, stories, highlights)

## Supported Content Types

The extension handles extraction from:
- Standard posts
- Reels
- Stories (current)
- Highlight stories
- TV content

## Implementation Strategy

Rather than hardcoding brittle CSS selectors, the tool leverages Instagram's frontend framework directly. This approach provides resilience against UI changes since it targets the underlying data structure rather than visual elements.

The actual JavaScript/TypeScript source files in `/src` would contain the regex patterns, ReactFiber traversal logic, and fetch API calls to retrieve media URLs at high resolution.

input.prompt: Show the complete source code for post/video extraction logic, especially how it gets video URLs from Instagram

content.content:
# Instagram Post/Video Extraction Logic

The code provided shows the complete extraction mechanism. Here's how it retrieves video URLs:

## Video Detection & URL Extraction

The `extractMediaData()` function identifies media type and extracts URLs accordingly:

```javascript
const isVideo = item['media_type'] !== 1;
const media = {
    url: isVideo ? item['video_versions'][0].url : item['image_versions2'].candidates[0].url,
    isVideo,
    id: item.pk
};
```

**Key points:**
- Videos are identified when `media_type` is not equal to 1
- Video URLs come from `item['video_versions'][0].url` 
- Image URLs come from `item['image_versions2'].candidates[0].url`
- Both carousel posts and single media are supported

## Data Flow

1. **Shortcode conversion**: Converts Instagram shortcodes to numeric post IDs using base-64 decoding
2. **API calls**: Fetches post data from `/api/v1/media/{postId}/info/`
3. **Fallback mechanism**: If initial request fails (400 status), uses GraphQL API to obtain correct post ID
4. **Media extraction**: Parses carousel arrays or single items, extracting URLs and metadata

The extracted data includes timestamps, usernames, media URLs, and type identification for downstream processing.

# Instagram Post/Video Extraction Logic

The code provided shows the complete extraction mechanism. Here's how it retrieves video URLs:

## Video Detection & URL Extraction

The `extractMediaData()` function identifies media type and extracts URLs accordingly:

```javascript
const isVideo = item['media_type'] !== 1;
const media = {
    url: isVideo ? item['video_versions'][0].url : item['image_versions2'].candidates[0].url,
    isVideo,
    id: item.pk
};
```

**Key points:**
- Videos are identified when `media_type` is not equal to 1
- Video URLs come from `item['video_versions'][0].url` 
- Image URLs come from `item['image_versions2'].candidates[0].url`
- Both carousel posts and single media are supported

## Data Flow

1. **Shortcode conversion**: Converts Instagram shortcodes to numeric post IDs using base-64 decoding
2. **API calls**: Fetches post data from `/api/v1/media/{postId}/info/`
3. **Fallback mechanism**: If initial request fails (400 status), uses GraphQL API to obtain correct post ID
4. **Media extraction**: Parses carousel arrays or single items, extracting URLs and metadata

The extracted data includes timestamps, usernames, media URLs, and type identification for downstream processing.

## Descoberta Importante

A extensão usa a API do Instagram diretamente:
- **Endpoint**: `/api/v1/media/{postId}/info/`
- **Vídeos**: `item['video_versions'][0].url`
- **Imagens**: `item['image_versions2'].candidates[0].url`
- **Identificação**: `media_type !== 1` = vídeo

Isso significa que em vez de interceptar requests de rede (que são fragmentados), devemos **chamar a API do Instagram diretamente** com o shortcode do reel.
